Because there may be many instances of Assembler that query the same external model, the binding of a query to a specific external model is separated into the ForeignModel class. Instances of this class behave like database connections.

EADefect.name: Need element in Foundations to represent business architecture model
EADefect.description: There is no element that binds together all of the elements and maps that comprise a business architecture model.
EADefect.subject: EAPK_0EF376B9_A55D_4f35_B716_3AB037B46488

EADefect.name: Need SMM Measure Library model element in Foundations
EADefect.description: A Model element will be needed to contain SMM measure libraries. Model elements may also be useful for holding alternative business architecture models. This issue may depend on the resolution of Issue 18033DF939ISS
EADefect.subject: EAPK_0EF376B9_A55D_4f35_B716_3AB037B46488

EADefect.name: No model elements that represents maps
EADefect.description: This issue is being raised against the Foundations package, but it probably applies to other packages. There is no model currently defined that represents the notion of a map. In addition, there is no provision for a library of maps. This issue depends on the resolution of Issue 18033DF939ISS.
EADefect.subject: EAPK_0EF376B9_A55D_4f35_B716_3AB037B46488

EADefect.name: Restriction of strategy change to operating model classes in the meta-model
EADefect.description: To avoid circularity, strategy driven change should be restricted to the "operating model" classes in the maeta-model. Currently, this is defined by the change target being any concrete subclass of the OperatingEntity abstract class. This does not permit change to apply to relationships defined by concrete subclasses of the RoleElement class. This could be resolved by adding a new subclass under RoleElement that would become subject to change by the strategy. However, the meta-model currently uses simple UML associations to represent relationships that would involve RoleElement concrete subclasses when translated into the actual MOF model. There is no simple way to add these associations to an explicit accounting of the model elements that are part of the operating model. A cleaner solution could be to define the change target as all concrete subclasses of BusinessElement except those in the Strategy package.
EADefect.subject: EAPK_0EF376B9_A55D_4f35_B716_3AB037B46488

EADefect.name: Add operations to navigate the query result set to Assembler
EADefect.subject: EAPK_1FC7AC1C_2DA9_45e7_A356_21464B5B953C

EADefect.name: Decomposition of BusinessSocialGroup
EADefect.description: The meta-model currently creates an organization hierarchy by way of Executive delegation. This does not allow decomposition to be used to define roles within a group without also defining Executives to be accountable for those roles. It also does not allow the creation of roles in CollaborativeTeams and Forums.
EADefect.subject: EAPK_325D14D7_1B3C_4e4e_A0A2_EB655EDC4484

EADefect.name: Need to define Organization Chart
EADefect.description: BusinessUnit is defined to have a place in the org chart. The meta-model does not represent the concept of an organization chart, so it is not possible to apply the definition to the BusinessUnit meta-model class by an association to an organization chart class.
EADefect.subject: EAPK_325D14D7_1B3C_4e4e_A0A2_EB655EDC4484

EADefect.name: Organigraphs, social networks and other forms of organization mapping not supported
EADefect.description: The BIZBOK describes a number of different organization mapping types. The meta-model currently supports only command and control maps. Consider the need for an GroupRelation RoleElement and a self-aggregation association to provide the capability for the business architect to model the additional information needed to create such maps (and to collect information about informal organization structures that would be useful for analysis).
EADefect.subject: EAPK_325D14D7_1B3C_4e4e_A0A2_EB655EDC4484

EADefect.name: Need a way to construct a measure that applies to a dynamically created n-ary association between multiple classifiers in the meta-model
EADefect.description: The meta-model should not have to define associations for every potential n-ary association of BusinessElements that an architect/analyst might want to create and associate a measurement with. SMM associates a measurement with a MOF classifier. SMM associates a Measure with a Scope - a collection of MOF classifiers. One solution is to define an n-ary association over BusinessElements as a special MOF classifier, a BusinessElementMeasureScope class. The analyst can then create such associations as needed in the instance model and define SMM measures for them. The instance model will typically not contain instances of instances of BusinessElement, so the measurements will be aggregations of the actual measurements that would be associated with those instances. For example, one could measure the customer satisfaction individual customers have with a particular Product. The customer satisfaction measure is scoped by a measure association between CustomerSegment and Product. Since neither individual customers nor individual products are part of the Business Architecture model, the measurement association would be between CustomerSegment (aggregate of the individual customers) and Product (aggregate of the individual products) and therefore is an aggregate like mean or median.
EADefect.subject: EAPK_379F3F8D_8E4F_402d_A2BE_29240AA13F45

EADefect.name: Resolve issues regarding service inclusion in Product
EADefect.description: 1. CapabilityCharacteristic and Capability both enable ProductConcept. Define what enablement means. 2.
EADefect.subject: EAPK_4D2D709B_EA95_4904_BDBF_29787B3559A1

EADefect.name: Executive sets Policy redundant
EADefect.description: Executive sets Policy appears to be redundant with Stakeholder sets Policy. Resolution may depend on Issue 1802D951F1ISS (Stakeholder class) being resolved. If Executive implies ParticipatingStakeholder then redundancy is real and should be removed.
EADefect.subject: EAPK_631CB4FE_0553_4458_825B_952B6DCA4CCC

EADefect.name: Should Stakeholder sets Policy be ParticipatingStakeholder sets Policy
EADefect.description: Stakeholder has a broad definition that includes passive recipients of value. The ability to set policy is more narrowly defined to stakeholders with authority to compel changes in the organization's behavior. ParticipatingStakeholder implies but does not explicitly state the ability to compel behavior.
EADefect.subject: EAPK_631CB4FE_0553_4458_825B_952B6DCA4CCC

EADefect.name: Strategy model element needed
EADefect.description: In the strategy package, there is no Strategy element to represent a strategy. Consequently, models will consist of collections of courses of action and objectives without anything to indicate that these may represent more than one strategy. This could be important if the business architect is asked to analyze alternative strategies, some of which may contain the same courses of action and objectives.
EADefect.subject: EAPK_7FB84CF6_7C43_4fcd_8EE2_597D3571CE37

EADefect.name: Entry and Exit criteria missing from meta-model
EADefect.description: These criteria are not defined in the meta-model. Semantically, they overlap with Outcomes and the "need" and "produce" relationships. Is there enough of a difference between entry/exit criteria and need/produce Outcome relationships to add the entry/exit criteria to the model.
EADefect.subject: EAPK_8F4BF952_A3B0_40fa_AB7B_0892B24E49F7

EADefect.name: Restriction of strategy change to operating model classes in the meta-model
EADefect.description: To avoid circularity, strategy driven change should be restricted to the "operating model" classes in the maeta-model. Currently, this is defined by the change target being any concrete subclass of the OperatingEntity abstract class. This does not permit change to apply to relationships defined by concrete subclasses of the RoleElement class. This could be resolved by adding a new subclass under RoleElement that would become subject to change by the strategy. However, the meta-model currently uses simple UML associations to represent relationships that would involve RoleElement concrete subclasses when translated into the actual MOF model. There is no simple way to add these associations to an explicit accounting of the model elements that are part of the operating model. A cleaner solution could be to define the change target as all concrete subclasses of BusinessElement except those in the Strategy package.
EADefect.subject: EAID_D8196C55_8FC2_416b_B625_96E69EA65D12

EADefect.name: Relationship between Stakeholder and Organization::Executive
EADefect.description: Stakeholder is the abstract base class for both participating stakeholder and market segment. Participating stakeholder - an internal or external individual or organization with a vested interest in achieving value through a particular outcome. Executive is a concrete subclass of BusinessSocialGroup with the ability to monitor business performance and control behaviors of workers. Is it plausible to assert that all Executives are ParticipatingStakeholders?
EADefect.subject: EAID_C075F7D3_E53B_4742_B944_F97380FE319D

EADefect.name: Move ParticipatingStakeholder to Stakeholder package from Value
EADefect.subject: EAID_D8CB56A4_8BA8_465f_A5AC_395B914F8115

EADefect.name: Consider relabeling to CapabilityBehavior
EADefect.description: CapabilityBehavior more clearly expresses the concept this class intends to represent.
EADefect.subject: EAID_C3EB40D4_9821_4736_BA2B_4979694A49CA

EADefect.name: External/internal BusinessSocialGroup a class or a category
EADefect.description: The current Organization diagram has orthogonal specializations of BusinessSocialGroup intended to be disjoint: Executive/Worker BusinessUnit/ExternalUnit At the instance model an external group of workers would be represented by two instances, one of Worker and the other of ExternalUnit, with no way to indicate that these model instances represent the same business entity. An alternative solution would be to have only BusinessSocialGroup without specialization and to use Categorization or binary attributes to designate the possible combinations of these characteristics. Another alternative solution would be to assume that the Executive/Worker distinction is important and use Categorization or a binary feature to indicate the external/internal status of the gtroup. This solution has the defect that groups containing both Executives and Workers could not be defined (BusinessSocialGroup is currently an abstract class). Note that the BIZBOK defines the term Business Unit as a logical element or segment of a company (such as accounting, production, marketing) representing a specific business function, and a definite place on the organization chart, under the domain of a manager. Also called department, division, or a functional area. In the value, capability and strategy sections of the BIZBOK, it is typically used to refer to a business segment with identified P&L. Business function is defined as a process or operation that is performed routinesly to carry out a part of the mission of an organization. BusinessUnit is a BusinessSocialGroup with a defined place in the organization chart. CollaborativeTeam is a BusinessSocialGroup created by two or more business units and having a defined set of shared principles and common goals. Left unsaid is that the purpose of such a group is to find solutions that span the collaborating business units. Collaborative teams exist until the solution proposals are found or the business units determine that the solutions are no longer needed or are not likely to be found.
EADefect.subject: EAID_15BA4C0A_285A_485f_9CAF_5B43A0E1847C

EADefect.name: Consider replacing this class with an SMM measure
EADefect.description: Consider replacing this class with a measure defined in a business architecture specialization of SMM
EADefect.subject: EAID_982AD0A6_E105_42e6_B41D_4F7CF46C9397

EADefect.name: Change label to "Worker"
EADefect.description: Perhaps "worker" or "performer" is a better name for this class.
EADefect.subject: EAID_A5021256_421B_4e17_8EB8_A4DE5CC39C8F

EADefect.name: ProductLine grouping rule makes Product similarity associations redundant
EADefect.description: ProductLine should define the rule used to group products together. Product has similarity associations "is complementary to" and "is similar to". The first of these associations indicates that products could be bundled, but are not. This association should probably be derived from analysis and not explicit in the model. The second association asserts the potential for grouping the products into a product line. It should also be as association derived from an analysis and not explicit in the meta-model.
EADefect.subject: EAID_434EDD35_6D09_4dd6_B40F_D2C913609834

EADefect.name: Find a home for Entitlement in the class hierarchy
EADefect.description: Entitlement is a new component of a product concept. It is a specialization of InformationConcept that typically has a BusinessObject representation.
EADefect.subject: EAID_D3C5B415_7CD0_4e5b_A942_5621A8300C8A

EADefect.name: Specializations of Assembler to define the type of the query language
EADefect.description: Should the meta-model define specific query languages (e.g. OQL, SPARQL, SQL) by specializing the Assembler class. This would require compatibility with the ForeignModel object, since that is where the query engine and model connection are defined.
EADefect.subject: EAID_D64BF38E_E157_4d61_9215_570E87A7B72F
